account.name: Mangrove
account.description: A digital asset ecosystem with built-in guardrails, delegate visibility & management, and creator monetization. AI agents are welcome too with our x402 API.

tweets.text: Building AI agents for digital asset markets? The infrastructure you need is at https://t.co/vATHPdyEyR. Try Pro free for 2 weeks: code PROTRIAL.

At Mangrove, non-custodial oversight is not an option you turn on. It is how the platform was built from day one. AI Copilot. Automated strategies. Embedded guardrails. And your assets always in your control. That is how intelligent investing should work.

Non-custodial architecture exists precisely because this problem was foreseeable. When markets never close and AI is moving faster than any human can, concentrated custody risk is not theoretical. It is the most important infrastructure decision in digital asset investing.

Now layer AI on top of a custodial structure. The platform holds your assets AND controls your strategy. If the AI makes a bad call, or the platform freezes, gets hacked, or goes under, you have no independent control over either your assets or your positions. That is not a risk.

In traditional finance, custodial made sense. Markets closed at 4pm. Trades settled in days. There was time to intervene if something went wrong. Digital assets operate 24/7. AI agents execute in milliseconds. The rules changed. Most platforms did not.

First, the basics. Custodial: the platform holds your assets. You trust them with custody, execution, and access. Non-custodial: you retain control. The platform provides tools and intelligence. Your assets stay yours. Simple enough. But here is where it gets important.
Non-custodial vs. custodial. Most people get this wrong. Here is why it matters more than ever in an AI-driven market:

Hot take: Legacy finance closes at 4pm, but digital assets don't. The infrastructure you trust with your portfolio should be built for the market that actually exists and runs 24/7, not the one that that stops at 5pm and on weekends.

Paper trading helps you stress-test two things at once: the strategy and the trader. It's where you spot weak exits, sloppy sizing, or rules that are too complicated to follow. We built paper trading into Mangrove so you can refine the rules before real money is involved. https://t.co/2TES70SRh6
